metadata.title:
LABOR COMMITTEE WAS


ACTING ON INSTRUCTIONS

pages.raw_text:
Regarding the frequent asser.
lions that the committee of fifteen
in its campaign was not represent.
ing organized labor and that some
of the members exceeded their
authority, The Press gives below
the real truth about this cam.
paign; it is official and exact and
is given to make it useless for any
further fancy lying to be done by
political organs seeking to bolster
up a crooked official clique.


On February % the Central La.
Dor council voted to demand in
creased wages for city laborers
and three police matrons. The
council also instructed the presi.
dent to appoint a committee of
fifteen to have full charge of the
campaign, to present labor's de-
mands to the council and to get up
a demonstration on March 8. The
chairman appointed the following
committee: w. Horn, w. j.
Coates, Byron Vickerage, Fred
Cardamone, ]. w Gimson, J. c.
Gordon, w. j. Sullivan, w. A.
Cliff, Thomas Ivey, w. R. Owen,
Robert Ronald, H. L. Hughes, d.
c. Coates, A. w. Swenson and H.
A. Jarvis.


That same evening this commit
tee met and chose ID. G. Coates as
chairman, he being absent at the
time. The committee also author
i'm, tn loh IA
mands to the city council, which
was done on that night of March
s, when he was elected by two de-
tectiveS at the request of the
president of the council.


But before, on the night before
March s, the committee reported
back to the labor council and re.
ceived not only its unanimous APP.
proval of the campaign as begun,
but instructions to go ahead and
do exactly what was done, even to
selecting the street speakers.


Then, on March IA, the commit
tee reported to the central council
again, and that body unanimously
ordered the committee to go ahead
and demand of the council an in
vestigating committee of seven
for the Jail probing and three po-
lice matrons, and these demands
also were presented by the COM
mittee to the council per instruc.
ton.


Finally, last night the council
placed for the fourth time its in
dorsement of every act of the com
mittee and of its chairman, thus
settling the various false reports
that had been maliciously circu-
lated by almost all labor leaders
working fox the city administra-
ton.


At no time since the campaign
began on the city hall has any
member of the committee of fiji
teen acted without the full knowl-
edge and authorization of the en-
